The mercata app (strato-platform/mercata/) is the complete reference:

  • Backend - mercata/backend/src/ - Shows all STRATO API patterns
  • Transaction Builder - mercata/backend/src/utils/txBuilder.ts
  • Transaction Helper - mercata/backend/src/utils/txHelper.ts
  • API Clients - mercata/backend/src/utils/mercataApiHelper.ts

docs.title: Architecture - STRATO Platform
docs.text: Understanding the STRATO platform monorepo structure and how components work together.

docs.title: Monorepo Overview
docs.text:
The STRATO platform is a monorepo containing:

  1. Layer 1: Blockchain (strato/) - Core blockchain (Haskell)
  2. Layer 2: DeFi Application (mercata/) - Application layer (Solidity, TypeScript, React)
  3. Supporting Infrastructure - Deployment, proxies, documentation
strato-platform/
├── strato/              # Blockchain core (Haskell)
├── mercata/             # DeFi application (TypeScript, Solidity, React)
├── bootstrap-docker/    # Production deployment scripts
├── nginx-packager/      # Reverse proxy & Swagger docs
├── docs/                # Additional documentation
└── techdocs/            # User & developer documentation (this site!)

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text: Custom EVM-compatible blockchain built on Haskell for security and performance.

docs.title: strato-core
docs.text:
What: Main blockchain node (consensus, state, networking)

Language: Haskell

Location: strato/core/

Responsibilities:

  • Block production and validation
  • Consensus mechanism
  • P2P networking
  • State management
  • Transaction pool

Key modules:

  • BlockApps.Blockchain - Block processing
  • BlockApps.Network - P2P networking
  • BlockApps.VM - VM interface
  • BlockApps.State - World state management

docs.title: vm-runner
docs.text:
What: EVM (Ethereum Virtual Machine) executor

Language: Haskell

Location: strato/vm-runner/

Responsibilities:

  • Execute smart contract bytecode
  • Gas metering
  • State transitions
  • Opcode implementations

docs.title: strato-api
docs.text:
What: JSON-RPC API server (Ethereum-compatible)

Language: Haskell

Location: strato/api/

Responsibilities:

  • HTTP/WebSocket API endpoints
  • Ethereum JSON-RPC compatibility
  • Transaction submission
  • Query interface

API endpoints:

  • /strato-api/eth/v1.2/account - Account management
  • /strato-api/eth/v1.2/transaction - Transaction submission
  • /strato-api/eth/v1.2/block - Block queries
  • /strato-api/eth/v1.2/contract - Contract interactions

docs.title: cirrus
docs.text:
What: Blockchain indexer (PostgreSQL-based)

Language: Haskell

Location: strato/cirrus/

Responsibilities:

  • Index blockchain data into PostgreSQL
  • Fast queries for transactions, events, balances
  • Block explorer data
  • Historical data access

Database schema:

  • blocks - Block data
  • transactions - Transaction data
  • events - Smart contract events
  • accounts - Account balances

docs.title: Data Flow (Layer 1)
docs.text:
User → JSON-RPC API → strato-core → vm-runner → State
                           ↓
                        cirrus → PostgreSQL
  1. User submits transaction via JSON-RPC
  2. strato-core validates and adds to mempool
  3. Block produced, transactions executed in vm-runner
  4. State updated
  5. cirrus indexes results to PostgreSQL

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text: Full-stack DeFi application built on top of STRATO blockchain.

docs.title: Smart Contracts (mercata/contracts/)
docs.text:
What: Solidity smart contracts for DeFi protocols

Language: Solidity 0.8.22

Location: mercata/contracts/concrete/

Core Protocols:

  1. Lending (Lending/)
  2. LendingPool.sol - Main lending logic
  3. CollateralVault.sol - Collateral storage

  4. Health factor calculations, liquidations

  5. CDP (Collateralized Debt Position) (CDP/)

  6. CDPEngine.sol - Mint USDST stablecoin
  7. CDPVault.sol - Collateral storage

  8. Stability fees, liquidation ratios

  9. AMM Pools (Pools/)

  10. Pool.sol - Automated market maker
  11. PoolFactory.sol - Pool creation

  12. Swap, add/remove liquidity

  13. Bridge (Bridge/)

  14. MercataBridge.sol - Cross-chain transfers

  15. Deposit/withdrawal workflows

  16. Rewards (Rewards/)

  17. Rewards.sol - Incentives controller for distributing Reward Points
  18. Tracks user activities (borrowing, liquidity, swaps, etc.)
  19. Calculates and distributes rewards based on emission rates

  20. Activity-based incentive system

  21. Tokens (Tokens/)

  22. Token.sol - Base token implementation
  23. TokenFactory.sol - Token creation (USDST, Reward Points, wrapped assets)
  24. TokenMetadata.sol - Token metadata management

Build:

cd mercata/contracts
npm install
npm run compile
npm test

Test:

npm test                    # All tests
npm run test:coverage       # With coverage

docs.title: Backend API (mercata/backend/)
docs.text:
What: Node.js REST API for DeFi operations

Language: TypeScript (Node.js + Express)

Location: mercata/backend/src/

Responsibilities:

  • High-level DeFi API endpoints
  • User authentication (OAuth 2.0)
  • Transaction bundling (e.g., approve + supply)
  • Event monitoring
  • Database queries (Cirrus)

Key modules:

  • api/routes/ - Express routes
  • api/services/ - Business logic
  • lending.service.ts - Lending operations
  • cdp.service.ts - CDP operations
  • swapping.service.ts - Swap/liquidity
  • bridge.service.ts - Bridge operations
  • api/middleware/ - Auth, validation
  • db/ - Database client (PostgreSQL via Cirrus)

API endpoints: (Available at /api/docs)

  • POST /api/lending/supply - Supply collateral
  • POST /api/lending/borrow - Borrow USDST
  • POST /api/cdp/deposit - Deposit for CDP
  • POST /api/cdp/mint - Mint USDST
  • POST /api/swap/execute - Swap tokens
  • POST /api/pool/add-liquidity - Add liquidity

Run:

cd mercata/backend
npm install
npm run dev              # Development mode
npm run build            # Production build
npm start                # Start production server

Test:

npm test                 # Unit tests
npm run test:integration # Integration tests
npm run test:e2e         # E2E tests

docs.title: Frontend UI (mercata/ui/)
docs.text:
What: React web application for DeFi interactions

Language: TypeScript (React + Vite)

Location: mercata/ui/src/

Responsibilities:

  • User interface for DeFi operations
  • Wallet connection (MetaMask, WalletConnect)
  • Transaction submission
  • Real-time updates
  • Data visualization

Key modules:

  • pages/ - Main pages
  • Borrow.tsx - Lending interface
  • Advanced.tsx - CDP minting, pools
  • SwapAsset.tsx - Token swaps
  • DepositsPage.tsx - Bridge in
  • WithdrawalsPage.tsx - Bridge out
  • Rewards.tsx - Rewards management
  • components/ - Reusable components
  • hooks/ - React hooks (API calls, wallet)
  • contexts/ - State management
  • utils/ - Helpers, formatters

Tech stack:

  • React 18
  • TypeScript
  • Vite (build tool)
  • ethers.js (Web3 library)
  • Material-UI (components)

Run:

cd mercata/ui
npm install
npm run dev              # Development server (port 3001)
npm run build            # Production build
npm run preview          # Preview production build

Test:

npm test                 # Component tests
npm run test:e2e         # E2E tests (Playwright)

docs.title: Background Services (mercata/services/)
docs.text:
What: Long-running services for async operations

Language: TypeScript (Node.js)

Key services:

  1. Bridge Service (bridge/)
  2. Monitors Ethereum for deposits
  3. Processes withdrawals
  4. Mints wrapped assets

  5. Issues transaction vouchers (10 per bridge-in)

  6. Oracle Service (oracle/)

  7. Fetches external price feeds
  8. Updates on-chain prices

  9. Data sources (Chainlink, CoinGecko, etc.)

  10. Voucher Service (voucher/)

  11. Manages transaction vouchers
  12. Mints vouchers on bridge-in
  13. Tracks voucher usage

Run:

cd mercata/services/bridge
npm install
npm run dev

docs.title: Data Flow (Layer 2)
docs.text:
User (Browser)
    ↓
Frontend UI (React) → Wallet (MetaMask)
    ↓                       ↓
Backend API (Node.js) ← Blockchain (via ethers.js)
    ↓
Smart Contracts (Solidity)
    ↓
STRATO Blockchain (Layer 1)

Example: Borrow USDST

  1. User clicks "Borrow" in UI
  2. Frontend calls POST /api/lending/borrow
  3. Backend bundles transactions:
  4. Approve token spending
  5. Call LendingPool.borrow()
  6. Backend submits to STRATO blockchain
  7. vm-runner executes contract
  8. State updated, event emitted
  9. cirrus indexes transaction
  10. Backend returns result to frontend
  11. UI updates with new balance

docs.title: bootstrap-docker
docs.text:
What: Production deployment scripts

Location: bootstrap-docker/

Contains:

  • strato-run.sh - Main deployment script
  • docker-compose.yml - Service definitions
  • strato - CLI wrapper

Usage:

cd bootstrap-docker
sudo ./strato-run.sh

docs.title: nginx-packager
docs.text:
What: Reverse proxy and API gateway

Location: nginx-packager/

Responsibilities:

  • Route /strato-api/* → strato-api (Layer 1)
  • Route /api/* → mercata-backend (Layer 2)
  • Route / → mercata-ui (frontend)
  • Serve Swagger UI at /docs and /api/docs

Configuration:

  • nginx.conf - Main config
  • swagger/swagger.yaml - API specification

docs.title: Makefiles
docs.text:
Root Makefile: Makefile

Targets:

make                     # Build everything
make strato-core         # Build blockchain core
make mercata-contracts   # Build smart contracts
make mercata-backend     # Build backend API
make mercata-ui          # Build frontend UI
make docker-compose      # Generate docker-compose files
make test                # Run all tests

docs.title: Stack (Haskell)
docs.text:
What: Build tool for Haskell components

Configuration: stack.yaml

Usage:

stack build              # Build Haskell code
stack test               # Run Haskell tests
stack exec strato-core   # Run compiled binary

docs.title: npm (Node.js)
docs.text:
What: Package manager for JavaScript/TypeScript

Usage:

cd mercata/backend
npm install              # Install dependencies
npm run build            # Compile TypeScript
npm test                 # Run tests

cd mercata/ui
npm install
npm run dev              # Start dev server

docs.title: Technology Stack Summary
docs.text: Component Language Framework Purpose strato-core Haskell Stack Blockchain core vm-runner Haskell Stack EVM executor cirrus Haskell Stack + PostgreSQL Indexer Smart Contracts Solidity 0.8.22 Hardhat DeFi protocols Backend API TypeScript Node.js + Express REST API Frontend UI TypeScript React + Vite Web app Services TypeScript Node.js Background tasks Deployment Bash + Docker Docker Compose Infrastructure

docs.title: Internal Communication
docs.text:
Blockchain → API:

  • Direct function calls (Haskell)

Blockchain → Cirrus:

  • Database writes (PostgreSQL)

Backend → Blockchain:

  • HTTP (JSON-RPC API)
  • Port: 8080

Frontend → Backend:

  • HTTP (REST API)
  • Port: 3000

Frontend → Blockchain:

  • Direct via ethers.js (JSON-RPC)
  • For read operations, gas estimation

docs.title: External Communication
docs.text:
User → Frontend:

  • HTTPS (web browser)
  • Port: 443 (production) or 3001 (dev)

Bridge Service → Ethereum L1:

  • JSON-RPC (Infura, Alchemy)
  • Monitors events, submits transactions

Oracle Service → Price Feeds:

  • HTTPS (Chainlink, CoinGecko APIs)

docs.title: Choose Your Focus Area
docs.text:
Detailed component-specific guides are coming soon. For now, see:

  1. Blockchain Core - Haskell codebase (strato/core/, strato/api/)
  2. Smart Contracts - Solidity contracts (mercata/contracts/)
  3. Backend API - Node.js/TypeScript (mercata/backend/)
  4. Frontend UI - React/TypeScript (mercata/ui/)
  5. Services - Background services (mercata/services/)

docs.title: General Workflow
docs.text:
1. Make changes in your area
  2. Build your component
  3. Run tests (unit, integration)
  4. Test locally (full stack)
  5. Submit PR (see Contributing)

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text:
Now that you understand the architecture:

  1. Choose your area of interest (blockchain, contracts, backend, frontend, services)
  2. Read the component-specific guide
  3. Set up your development environment (see Setup)
  4. Make your first contribution (see Contributing)

docs.title: Need Help?
docs.text:
• Documentation: docs.strato.nexus
  • Support: support.blockapps.net
  • Telegram: t.me/strato_net

docs.title: Contributing to STRATO
docs.text: Guidelines for contributing to the STRATO platform codebase.

docs.title: Welcome Contributors!
docs.text: Thank you for your interest in contributing to STRATO! Whether you're fixing a bug, adding a feature, or improving documentation, your contribution is valued.

docs.title: 1. Set Up Your Development Environment
docs.text: Follow the Setup Guide to get STRATO running locally.

docs.title: 2. Understand the Architecture
docs.text: Read the Architecture Guide to understand how components work together.

docs.title: 3. Choose Your Area
docs.text:
Pick the area you want to work on:

  • Blockchain Core - Haskell (strato/)
  • Smart Contracts - Solidity (mercata/contracts/)
  • Backend API - Node.js/TypeScript (mercata/backend/)
  • Frontend UI - React/TypeScript (mercata/ui/)
  • Services - Background services (mercata/services/)

docs.title: 1. Find or Create an Issue
docs.text:
Option A: Work on Existing Issue

  1. Browse GitHub Issues
  2. Look for issues labeled:
  3. good-first-issue - Great for newcomers
  4. help-wanted - Community contributions welcome
  5. bug - Bug fixes
  6. enhancement - New features
  7. Comment on the issue to let others know you're working on it

Option B: Propose a New Feature

  1. Open a new issue
  2. Describe the feature/bug
  3. Wait for feedback from maintainers
  4. Once approved, start working on it

docs.title: 2. Fork and Clone (External Contributors)
docs.text:
If you're not a core team member:

# Fork the repo on GitHub first
git clone git@github.com:YOUR-USERNAME/strato-platform.git
cd strato-platform

# Add upstream remote
git remote add upstream git@github.com:blockapps/strato-platform.git

docs.title: 3. Create a Feature Branch
docs.text:
# Update main
git checkout main
git pull upstream main

# Create feature branch
git checkout -b feature/your-feature-name

Branch Naming Convention:

  • feature/description - New features
  • fix/description - Bug fixes
  • docs/description - Documentation changes
  • refactor/description - Code refactoring
  • test/description - Test improvements

Examples:

  • feature/add-liquidation-alerts
  • fix/lending-pool-health-factor
  • docs/update-api-examples

docs.title: 4. Make Your Changes
docs.text:
Component-specific guidelines:

See the Architecture Guide for details on each component's structure and technology stack.

General guidelines:

  • Write clean, readable code
  • Follow existing code style
  • Add comments for complex logic
  • Update documentation if needed

docs.title: 5. Test Your Changes
docs.text:
Run tests:

# Full stack
make test

# Component-specific
cd mercata/contracts && npm test
cd mercata/backend && npm test
cd mercata/ui && npm test

Test locally:

./start test_node
# Manually test your changes
./forceWipe
rm -rf test_node/

Ensure:

  • All existing tests pass
  • New features have tests
  • No regressions introduced

docs.title: 6. Commit Your Changes
docs.text:
Commit Message Format:

<type>(<scope>): <subject>

<body>

<footer>

Types:

  • feat - New feature
  • fix - Bug fix
  • docs - Documentation
  • style - Formatting (no code change)
  • refactor - Code refactoring
  • test - Adding/updating tests
  • chore - Build/tooling changes

Examples:

feat(lending): add liquidation alerts

Add email and webhook notifications when positions approach
liquidation threshold. Users can configure alert levels
in the UI settings.

Closes #123

fix(cdp): correct stability fee calculation

The rate accumulator was not being updated correctly,
causing incorrect interest accrual. Fixed by updating
the accumulator on every state change.

Fixes #456

docs(api): update REST API examples

Update all API examples to use the new authentication
flow with OAuth 2.0 tokens.

Commit Best Practices:

  • One logical change per commit
  • Write clear, descriptive messages
  • Reference issue numbers (Closes #123, Fixes #456)
  • Keep commits atomic (easy to revert if needed)

docs.title: 7. Push Your Branch
docs.text: git push origin feature/your-feature-name

docs.title: 8. Open a Pull Request
docs.text:
1. Go to GitHub
  2. Click "New Pull Request"
  3. Select your branch
  4. Fill out the PR template (see below)
  5. Submit

PR Title Format:

Same as commit messages: <type>(<scope>): <subject>

PR Description Template:

## Description
Brief description of what this PR does.

## Motivation
Why is this change needed? What problem does it solve?

## Changes
- List of changes made
- Breaking changes (if any)
- New dependencies (if any)

## Testing
How was this tested? Steps to reproduce:
1. Step 1
2. Step 2
3. Expected result

## Checklist
- [ ] Tests pass locally
- [ ] New tests added (if applicable)
- [ ] Documentation updated (if applicable)
- [ ] No breaking changes (or documented)
- [ ] Code follows style guidelines

## Related Issues
Closes #123
Fixes #456

docs.title: Haskell (Blockchain Core)
docs.text:
Style:

  • Use stylish-haskell for formatting
  • 2-space indentation
  • Max line length: 100 characters
  • Explicit type signatures
  • Descriptive function names

Example:

-- Good
processBlock :: Block -> StateT BlockchainState IO (Either BlockError ())
processBlock block = do
  validateBlock block
  executeTransactions (blockTransactions block)
  updateState block

-- Bad (no type signature, unclear name)
pb b = do
  validateBlock b
  executeTransactions (blockTransactions b)
  updateState b

docs.title: Solidity (Smart Contracts)
docs.text:
Style:

  • Use prettier-plugin-solidity for formatting
  • 4-space indentation
  • Follow Solidity Style Guide
  • Explicit visibility modifiers
  • NatSpec comments for public functions

Example:

// Good
/// @notice Supply collateral to the lending pool
/// @param asset The address of the asset to supply
/// @param amount The amount to supply
/// @return success True if supply was successful
function supplyCollateral(
    address asset,
    uint256 amount
) external returns (bool success) {
    require(amount > 0, "Amount must be greater than 0");
    // ... implementation
}

// Bad (no comments, missing validation)
function supply(address a, uint b) public returns (bool) {
    // ... implementation
}

docs.title: TypeScript (Backend & Frontend)
docs.text:
Style:

  • Use ESLint + Prettier
  • 2-space indentation
  • Max line length: 100 characters
  • Explicit types (avoid any)
  • Descriptive names

Example:

// Good
async function borrowUSDST(
  user: string,
  asset: string,
  amount: bigint
): Promise<TransactionResult> {
  const healthFactor = await calculateHealthFactor(user);
  if (healthFactor < MIN_HEALTH_FACTOR) {
    throw new Error("Health factor too low");
  }
  // ... implementation
}

// Bad (no types, unclear name)
async function borrow(u, a, amt) {
  // ... implementation
}

docs.title: General Guidelines
docs.text:
DRY (Don't Repeat Yourself):

  • Extract common logic into functions
  • Use libraries for shared code

KISS (Keep It Simple, Stupid):

  • Simple solutions over clever ones
  • Clear code over comments

Error Handling:

  • Always handle errors gracefully
  • Provide helpful error messages
  • Log errors for debugging

Security:

  • Never commit secrets (API keys, private keys)
  • Validate all inputs
  • Follow security best practices for your language

docs.title: What to Expect
docs.text:
1. Automated Checks: CI/CD will run tests and linters
  2. Maintainer Review: A core team member will review your code
  3. Feedback: You may be asked to make changes
  4. Approval: Once approved, your PR will be merged

docs.title: Review Timeline
docs.text:
• Simple fixes: 1-2 days
  • Features: 3-7 days
  • Large changes: 1-2 weeks

docs.title: Addressing Feedback
docs.text:
# Make requested changes
git add .
git commit -m "Address review feedback"
git push origin feature/your-feature-name

Tips:

  • Respond to all comments
  • Ask questions if unclear
  • Be open to feedback
  • Iterate quickly

docs.title: Unit Tests
docs.text:
What: Test individual functions/components in isolation

When: Every new function/method

Example (TypeScript):

describe('calculateHealthFactor', () => {
  it('should return correct health factor for safe position', () => {
    const result = calculateHealthFactor(
      BigInt('10000'), // collateral
      BigInt('5000'),  // debt
      8500            // liquidation threshold
    );
    expect(result).toBe(BigInt('17000'));
  });
});

docs.title: Integration Tests
docs.text:
What: Test multiple components working together

When: New features involving multiple modules

Example:

describe('Lending Flow', () => {
  it('should allow supply, borrow, repay, and withdraw', async () => {
    await supplyCollateral(user, 'ETHST', ethers.parseEther('10'));
    await borrow(user, 'USDST', ethers.parseEther('5000'));
    await repay(user, 'USDST', ethers.parseEther('5000'));
    await withdrawCollateral(user, 'ETHST', ethers.parseEther('10'));
  });
});

docs.title: E2E Tests
docs.text:
What: Test full user workflows from UI to blockchain

When: Major features, critical flows

Example:

test('User can borrow USDST', async ({ page }) => {
  await page.goto('http://localhost:3001/borrow');
  await page.click('[data-testid="supply-button"]');
  await page.fill('[data-testid="amount-input"]', '10');
  await page.click('[data-testid="confirm-button"]');
  await expect(page.locator('[data-testid="success-message"]')).toBeVisible();
});

docs.title: When to Update Documentation
docs.text:
Always update docs when you:

  • Add a new feature
  • Change existing behavior
  • Fix a bug that was documented incorrectly
  • Add/remove API endpoints
  • Change configuration options

docs.title: What to Update
docs.text:
• User docs: techdocs/ (this site)
  • API docs: Swagger/OpenAPI specs
  • Code comments: In-line comments for complex logic
  • README files: Component-specific README files

docs.title: Common Issues
docs.text:
Build fails:

# Clean and rebuild
make clean
make

Tests fail:

# Wipe and restart
./forceWipe
rm -rf test_node/
./start test_node
make test

Merge conflicts:

# Update from main
git checkout main
git pull upstream main
git checkout feature/your-feature-name
git rebase main
# Resolve conflicts
git rebase --continue
git push origin feature/your-feature-name --force

docs.title: Getting Help
docs.text:
Questions?

  • Documentation: docs.strato.nexus
  • Support: support.blockapps.net
  • Telegram: t.me/strato_net

Stuck on a contribution?

  • Comment on the GitHub issue
  • Ask in Telegram
  • Reach out to maintainers

docs.title: Thank You!
docs.text:
Your contributions make STRATO better for everyone. We appreciate your time and effort!

Next Steps:

  • Setup - Get started
  • Architecture - Understand the system

docs.title: Setup - Contributing to STRATO
docs.text: This guide covers everything you need to start contributing to the STRATO platform codebase.

docs.title: Who is This For?
docs.text:
You're in the right place if you want to:

  • Contribute to the STRATO blockchain core (Haskell)
  • Develop smart contracts for the DeFi layer (Solidity)
  • Work on the backend API (Node.js/TypeScript)
  • Build the frontend UI (React/TypeScript)
  • Improve infrastructure and services

Not what you're looking for?

  • Building apps that use STRATO? → See Building Apps on STRATO
  • Running your own node? → See Node Operators (coming soon)

docs.title: 1. Stack (Haskell Build Tool)
docs.text:
What: Build tool for Haskell code (blockchain core)

Install: https://docs.haskellstack.org/en/stable/install_and_upgrade/

curl -sSL https://get.haskellstack.org/ | sh

Verify:

stack --version

docs.title: 2. Docker with Compose Plugin
docs.text:
What: Runtime environment for running STRATO components

Install: https://docs.docker.com/engine/install/

Verify:

docker --version
docker compose version

docs.title: 3. Node.js 18+ and npm
docs.text:
What: For building frontend and backend components

Install: https://nodejs.org/ or use nvm

# Using nvm (recommended)
curl -o- https://raw.githubusercontent.com/nvm-sh/nvm/v0.39.0/install.sh | bash
nvm install 18
nvm use 18

Verify:

node --version  # Should be v18.x or higher
npm --version

docs.title: 4. System Libraries
docs.text:
You have two options:

Option A: Install System-wide

Ubuntu 24.04:

sudo apt install -y \
  libleveldb-dev \
  liblzma-dev \
  libpq-dev \
  libsecp256k1-dev \
  libsodium-dev \
  postgresql-client

macOS (requires Homebrew):

brew install --quiet \
  leveldb \
  postgresql \
  libsodium \
  pkg-config \
  secp256k1 \
  xz

Option B: Use Nix (Recommended for consistency)

# Install Nix
curl -L https://nixos.org/nix/install | sh

# Nix packages are predefined in the project
# No manual library installation needed

Verify:

# Check if libraries are available
pkg-config --modversion leveldb
pkg-config --modversion libsodium

docs.title: 5. OAuth Client Credentials
docs.text:
What: Required to connect to STRATO network

How to get:

  1. Go to support.blockapps.net
  2. Sign in
  3. Click "Request Client Credentials"
  4. You'll receive:
  5. OAUTH_DISCOVERY_URL
  6. OAUTH_CLIENT_ID
  7. OAUTH_CLIENT_SECRET

Keep these safe! You'll need them to run STRATO locally.

docs.title: 1. Clone the STRATO Platform Monorepo
docs.text:
git clone git@github.com:blockapps/strato-platform.git
cd strato-platform

What's included:

strato-platform/
├── strato/              # Blockchain core (Haskell)
├── mercata/             # DeFi application layer
│   ├── contracts/       # Smart contracts (Solidity)
│   ├── backend/         # REST API (Node.js/TypeScript)
│   ├── ui/              # Web UI (React/TypeScript)
│   └── services/        # Background services
├── bootstrap-docker/    # Production deployment
├── nginx-packager/      # Reverse proxy
└── docs/                # Documentation

docs.title: 2. Install Git Hooks (Optional but Recommended)
docs.text:
Pre-commit hook: Automatically removes trailing whitespace

cp scripts/hooks/pre-commit .git/hooks/pre-commit
chmod +x .git/hooks/pre-commit

docs.title: Option 1: Build Everything
docs.text:
First Time Building?

The first build will take 15-30 minutes as Stack downloads dependencies and compiles the Haskell codebase. Subsequent builds are much faster (2-5 minutes for incremental changes).

With system-wide libraries:

make

With Nix:

NIX=true make

What this does:

  • Compiles the Haskell blockchain core (strato, vm-runner, cirrus)
  • Builds the Solidity smart contracts (mercata/contracts)
  • Builds the Node.js backend API (mercata/backend)
  • Builds the React frontend UI (mercata/ui)
  • Generates Docker Compose files

Build output:

✓ strato-core
✓ vm-runner
✓ cirrus
✓ mercata-contracts
✓ mercata-backend
✓ mercata-ui
✓ docker-compose files generated

docs.title: Option 2: Build Individual Components
docs.text:
If you're only working on a specific part:

Backend only:

make mercata-backend

Frontend only:

make mercata-ui

Contracts only:

cd mercata/contracts
npm install
npm run compile

Blockchain core only:

make strato-core

docs.title: Option 3: Generate Docker Compose Files Only
docs.text:
make docker-compose

This overwrites existing docker-compose.yml files with the latest configuration.

docs.title: 1. Build STRATO
docs.text:
cd strato-platform
make

What this does:

  • Compiles the Haskell blockchain core
  • Builds Solidity smart contracts
  • Builds Node.js backend API
  • Builds React frontend UI

Time: First build takes 15-30 minutes, subsequent builds are 2-5 minutes.

docs.title: 2. Start STRATO
docs.text:
./start my_node_name

What this does:

  • Starts all STRATO components (blockchain, API, UI, services)
  • Creates a local node directory (my_node_name/)
  • Blockchain data stored in my_node_name/strato-data/
  • Logs in my_node_name/logs/

First-time setup:

The first run will:

  1. Initialize the blockchain database
  2. Deploy core contracts
  3. Start API servers
  4. Launch the UI

This takes 2-5 minutes.

docs.title: 3. Verify It's Running
docs.text:
Check the blockchain API:

curl http://localhost:8080/strato-api/eth/v1.2/account

Expected response:

{
  "accounts": []
}

Check the REST API:

curl http://localhost:3000/api/health

Expected response:

{
  "status": "healthy"
}

Check the UI:

Open your browser: http://localhost:3001

You should see the STRATO DeFi interface.

docs.title: 4. Access Logs
docs.text:
All logs:

tail -f my_node_name/logs/*.log

Blockchain core:

tail -f my_node_name/logs/strato-core.log

Backend API:

tail -f my_node_name/logs/mercata-backend.log

UI:

tail -f my_node_name/logs/mercata-ui.log

docs.title: 5. Stop STRATO
docs.text:
# Graceful shutdown
./stop

# Force stop
pkill -f strato

docs.title: 6. Wipe and Start Fresh
docs.text:
./forceWipe
rm -rf my_node_name/
./start my_node_name

Wipe Deletes All Data

This removes all blockchain data, contracts, and transactions. Use only for testing/development.

docs.title: Run in Docker (Production-like)
docs.text: For a fully Dockerized setup (similar to production):

docs.title: 1. Prepare Docker Compose
docs.text:
cp docker-compose.allDocker.yml bootstrap-docker/docker-compose.yml
cd bootstrap-docker

docs.title: 2. Configure Environment
docs.text:
Edit strato-run.sh with your credentials:

NODE_HOST='localhost' \
network='helium' \
OAUTH_CLIENT_ID='your-client-id-here' \
OAUTH_CLIENT_SECRET='your-client-secret-here' \
./strato

Network options:

  • network='helium' - Testnet
  • network='upquark' - Mainnet

docs.title: 3. Start
docs.text:
sudo ./strato-run.sh

This will:

  • Pull Docker images
  • Start all services in containers
  • Expose ports (8080, 3000, 3001)

docs.title: 4. Wipe Docker Deployment
docs.text:
cd bootstrap-docker
sudo ./strato --wipe

docs.title: Build Failures
docs.text:
Problem: stack: command not found

Solution: Install Stack:

curl -sSL https://get.haskellstack.org/ | sh

Problem: Could not find module 'Crypto.Secp256k1'

Solution: Install system libraries:

# Ubuntu
sudo apt install libsecp256k1-dev

# macOS
brew install secp256k1

Problem: cabal: Missing dependency on a foreign library: * Missing (or bad) C library: leveldb

Solution: Install leveldb:

# Ubuntu
sudo apt install libleveldb-dev

# macOS
brew install leveldb

docs.title: Runtime Errors
docs.text:
Problem: Port already in use (8080, 3000, 3001)

Solution:

# Find and kill processes
lsof -ti:8080 | xargs kill -9
lsof -ti:3000 | xargs kill -9
lsof -ti:3001 | xargs kill -9

Problem: Database connection failed

Solution: Ensure PostgreSQL is running:

# Check status
docker ps | grep postgres

# Restart
docker-compose restart postgres

Problem: OAuth authentication failed

Solution: Verify your credentials in strato-run.sh or request new ones at support.blockapps.net.

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text:
Now that you have STRATO running locally:

  1. Understand the architecture → Read Architecture
  2. Choose your focus area:
  3. Blockchain Core - Haskell codebase (strato/)
  4. Smart Contracts - Solidity contracts (mercata/contracts/)
  5. Backend API - Node.js/TypeScript (mercata/backend/)
  6. Frontend UI - React/TypeScript (mercata/ui/)
  7. Services - Background services (mercata/services/)
  8. Make your first contribution → Read Contributing Guidelines

Component Guides Coming Soon

Detailed guides for each component area will be added in future updates.

docs.title: Need Help?
docs.text:
• Documentation: docs.strato.nexus
  • Support: support.blockapps.net
  • Telegram: t.me/strato_net

docs.title: Borrow USDST
docs.text:
Access USD liquidity without selling your crypto assets.

Alternative: Mint via CDP

You can also mint USDST via CDP which typically has lower fees but requires more active management.

Variable Parameters

Interest rates, gas costs, and asset parameters shown are typical examples. Actual values may vary based on network conditions, asset type, and governance settings. Always check current rates in the app before transacting.

docs.title: Complete Example: Borrow $1,000 USDST
docs.text:
Your situation:

  • You have: 1 ETHST in your wallet
  • ETHST price: $3,000
  • You need: $1,000 USDST for expenses

What you'll do:

  1. Supply 1 ETHST as collateral
  2. Borrow 1,000 USDST
  3. Use your USDST
  4. Repay the loan (anytime)
  5. Withdraw your collateral

Time needed: 5 minutes Total gas cost: ~$0.30 (3 transactions) Interest cost: ~$4 per month (5% annual rate)

docs.title: Step 1: Supply Collateral
docs.text:
What you have:

  • 1 ETHST worth $3,000

In the app:

  1. Go to Borrow page (in sidebar)
  2. In the Collateral Management table, find ETHST
  3. Click the "Supply" button for ETHST 4. In the modal:
  4. Enter amount: 1.0 (or click "Max")
  5. Review the health impact preview
  6. Click "Supply"
  7. Confirm in wallet (~$0.10 gas)
  8. Approval + supply happen automatically in one transaction
  9. Wait 1-2 seconds

Result: 

✅ Collateral supplied: 1 ETHST ($3,000)
✅ Can borrow up to: $2,250 USDST (75% of collateral)
✅ Health Factor: N/A (no debt yet)

Your wallet:

  • Before: 1 ETHST
  • After: 0 ETHST (moved to collateral vault)

docs.title: Step 2: Borrow USDST
docs.text:
What you want: $1,000 USDST

In the app:

  1. Go to Borrow section → Click "Borrow"
  2. Enter amount: Type 1000 USDST

  3. Review the preview:

  4. Borrowing: 1,000 USDST

  5. New Health Factor: 1.8 (Safe ✓)
  6. Interest Rate: ~5% annually
  7. Max you could borrow: $2,250
  8. Click "Borrow"
  9. Confirm in wallet (~$0.10 gas)
  10. Wait 1-2 seconds

Result: 

✅ Borrowed: 1,000 USDST
✅ Health Factor: 1.8 (Safe)
✅ Your wallet: +1,000 USDST

Your position now:

  • Collateral: 1 ETHST ($3,000)
  • Debt: 1,000 USDST
  • Health Factor: 1.8 (very safe)
  • Still available to borrow: $1,250 more (but don't!)

What is Health Factor? - Above 2.0: Very safe ✅ - 1.5 - 2.0: Safe with buffer - 1.0 - 1.5: Moderate risk ⚠️ - Below 1.0: Liquidation danger ❌

Your 1.8 health factor means you have a good safety buffer.

docs.title: Step 3: Use Your USDST
docs.text:
You now have 1,000 USDST to use for:

  • ✅ Transaction fees on STRATO
  • ✅ Swap for other tokens
  • ✅ Provide liquidity
  • ✅ Bridge to other chains
  • ✅ Any other purpose

Your debt grows slowly:

  • Interest: ~5% per year = 0.014% per day
  • After 1 day: Owe $1,000.14
  • After 30 days: Owe ~$1,004
  • After 1 year: Owe ~$1,050

docs.title: Step 4: Repay (Anytime)
docs.text:
When you're ready (no rush, but interest accumulates):

  1. Get USDST to repay
  2. You might have it from what you borrowed
  3. Or swap other tokens for USDST

  4. Or mint more via CDP

  5. Go to Borrow page (in sidebar) → "Repay"

  6. Enter amount:

  7. Type specific amount (e.g., 1004 to repay all)

  8. Or click "Repay Max" to close loan completely
  9. Click "Repay"
  10. Confirm in wallet (~$0.10 gas)
  11. Wait 1-2 seconds

Result after full repayment: 

✅ Debt repaid: 1,004 USDST (1,000 principal + 4 interest)
✅ Health Factor: N/A (no debt)
✅ Gas cost: ~$0.10

Your position:

  • Collateral: 1 ETHST (still in vault)
  • Debt: 0 USDST
  • Health Factor: No debt
  • You can now withdraw collateral

docs.title: Step 5: Withdraw Collateral
docs.text:
After repaying fully:

  1. Go to Borrow page (in sidebar)
  2. In the Collateral Management table, find your asset
  3. Click the "Withdraw" button for that asset
  4. Select asset: Choose ETH
  5. Enter amount: Type 1.0 (or click "Max")
  6. Click "Withdraw Collateral"
  7. Confirm in wallet (~$0.10 gas)
  8. Wait 1-2 seconds

Result: 

✅ Withdrawn: 1 ETHST to your wallet
✅ Total position closed

Final accounting:

  • You borrowed: 1,000 USDST
  • You repaid: 1,004 USDST
  • Total cost: $4 interest + $0.30 gas = $4.30 total
  • You still have: 1 ETHST (same as you started)

docs.title: Scenario: ETHST Drops to $2,500
docs.text:
What happens:

  • Your collateral value: Now $2,500 (was $3,000)
  • Your debt: Still 1,000 USDST (unchanged)
  • Your health factor: Drops to 1.5 (caution ⚠️)

What to do:

  • Option 1: Add more collateral - Supply 0.2 more ETHST - Option 2: Repay some debt - Repay 400 USDST
  • Option 3: Monitor closely - Still safe, but watch the price

docs.title: Scenario: ETHST Drops to $2,000 (Danger!)
docs.text:
What happens:

  • Your collateral value: Now $2,000
  • Your debt: Still 1,000 USDST
  • Your health factor: 1.0 (liquidation risk ❌)

Danger zone:

  • Health factor below 1.0 = you can be liquidated
  • Liquidators can repay your debt and take your collateral
  • You lose 5-10% of collateral value as liquidation bonus

What to do immediately:

  1. Add more collateral, OR
  2. Repay some/all debt
  3. Don't let health factor drop below 1.0!

Best practice: Keep health factor above 2.0 for safety buffer.

docs.title: Check Your Position
docs.text:
In the app:

  • Go to Borrow page (in sidebar)

  • You'll see:

  • Collateral amount and value

  • Debt amount (with accrued interest)
  • Health Factor with color indicator
  • Available to borrow or withdraw

Health Factor colors:

  • 🟢 Green (> 2.0): Safe
  • 🟡 Yellow (1.5-2.0): Caution
  • 🟠 Orange (1.0-1.5): Warning
  • 🔴 Red (< 1.0): Danger - liquidation imminent

docs.title: Adding More Collateral
docs.text:
If health factor drops:

  1. Go to Supply
  2. Add more collateral
  3. Health factor improves immediately

docs.title: Partial Repayment
docs.text:
Don't need to repay all at once:

  1. Go to Repay
  2. Enter any amount to repay
  3. Reduces debt and improves health factor

docs.title: DO ✅
docs.text:
• Over-collateralize: Supply 2-3x what you plan to borrow
  • Monitor daily: Check health factor when prices move
  • Set alerts: Use price alerts for your collateral assets
  • Keep buffer: Maintain health factor above 2.0
  • Start small: Test with small amounts first
  • Save USDST: Keep some USDST for gas fees

docs.title: DON'T ❌
docs.text:
• Max out: Don't borrow your maximum capacity
  • Ignore warnings: Yellow/orange health factor = take action
  • Forget interest: Debt grows daily (track it)
  • Use all crypto: Keep some assets liquid
  • Panic sell: Add collateral instead during dips
  • Forget gas: Always have USDST for fees

docs.title: "Insufficient collateral"
docs.text:
Problem: Trying to borrow more than your collateral allows

Solution:

  1. Supply more collateral first, OR
  2. Reduce the borrow amount

docs.title: "Would exceed health factor limit"
docs.text:
Problem: This borrow would make your health factor too low

Solution:

  • Reduce borrow amount
  • Supply more collateral
  • Check your calculation: Can borrow up to 75% of collateral value

docs.title: "Insufficient USDST balance"
docs.text:
Problem: Don't have enough USDST to repay

Solution:

  1. Swap other tokens for USDST, OR
  2. Mint USDST via CDP, OR
  3. Repay a smaller amount now, rest later

docs.title: "Approval needed" or "Insufficient allowance"
docs.text:
Problem: Token approval failed in the bundled transaction

Solution:

  1. Try the supply operation again
  2. Ensure you have enough gas for the transaction
  3. Wait for confirmation
  4. Then retry your action

docs.title: Health factor dropping
docs.text:
Problem: Your collateral value is decreasing

Solution (act quickly):

  1. Add collateral: Supply more assets
  2. Repay debt: Even partial repayment helps
  3. Monitor closely: Set price alerts
  4. Don't wait: Act before it reaches 1.0

docs.title: Interest Rates
docs.text:
How interest works:

  • Interest accrues every second
  • Typical rate: ~5% annually
  • Compounds continuously

Example costs: | Borrowed | Time | Interest Owed | |----------|------|---------------| | $1,000 | 1 day | $0.14 | | $1,000 | 1 week | $1 | | $1,000 | 30 days | $4 | | $1,000 | 1 year | $50 | | $10,000 | 30 days | $40 |

docs.title: Gas Fees
docs.text:
Action Gas Cost Supply collateral ~$0.10 Borrow ~$0.10 Repay ~$0.10 Withdraw ~$0.10

Total for complete cycle: ~$0.30-$0.40

docs.title: Choose Borrowing (Lending Pool) If:
docs.text:
• ✅ Short-term liquidity need (days/weeks)
  • ✅ Want flexibility to add/remove collateral easily
  • ✅ Comfortable with variable rates
  • ✅ Need quick access

docs.title: Choose Minting (CDP) If:
docs.text:
• ✅ Long-term position (months)
  • ✅ Want lower, more stable fees
  • ✅ Maximizing capital efficiency
  • ✅ Willing to manage vaults

See: Mint USDST via CDP Guide

docs.title: Earn While You Have USDST
docs.text:
• Provide Liquidity - Earn fees on your USDST
  • Swap Tokens - Trade for other assets
  • Earn Rewards - Claim Reward Points

docs.title: Learn More
docs.text:
• Core Concepts - Health Factor, liquidation, etc.
  • Safety Guide - Risk management
  • FAQ - Common questions

docs.title: Need Help?
docs.text:
• Support: support.blockapps.net
  • Telegram: t.me/strato_net
  • Docs: docs.strato.nexus

docs.title: Bridge Assets
docs.text:
Transfer assets between Ethereum and STRATO networks.

Variable Timing & Costs

Bridge timing, gas costs, and confirmation times shown are typical estimates. Actual values may vary significantly based on network congestion, block confirmation times, and current gas prices. Ethereum gas fees can range from $5-100+ depending on network conditions.

docs.title: Complete Example: Bridge 0.5 ETH to STRATO
docs.text:
Your situation:

  • You have: 0.5 ETH on Ethereum mainnet
  • You want: Use it on STRATO for DeFi

What you'll do:

  1. Initiate bridge from Ethereum
  2. Wait for bridge processing (~10-15 min)
  3. Receive ETHST on STRATO
  4. Start using it

Time needed: 15-20 minutes Total cost: ~$15-30 Ethereum gas + $0.10 STRATO gas

docs.title: Quick Walkthrough
docs.text:
Step 1: Initiate Bridge - Go to Deposits (in sidebar) → Bridge In tab (for deposits) OR Withdrawals (in sidebar) for withdrawals - From: Ethereum → To: STRATO - Asset: ETH - Amount: 0.5 - Review: Gas ~$20 (Ethereum), STRATO fee ~$0.10

Step 2: Approve & Confirm - Click "Bridge" - Confirm in wallet (pay Ethereum gas) - Transaction submitted

Step 3: Wait for Processing - Ethereum confirmation: 1-2 minutes - Bridge processing: 10-15 minutes - Track status in Bridge page

Step 4: Receive on STRATO - Assets arrive automatically - Check STRATO wallet

Result: 

✅ Bridged: 0.5 ETH from Ethereum to STRATO
✅ Time: ~15 minutes total
✅ Cost: ~$20 Ethereum gas + $0.10 STRATO fee
✅ Received: 10 FREE transaction vouchers
✅ Ready to use on STRATO

Your wallet:

  • Ethereum: 0.5 ETH less
  • STRATO: 0.5 ETHST more (now usable for DeFi)
  • STRATO: 10 vouchers (for free transactions)

Free Transactions

Every time you bridge assets to STRATO, you receive 10 free transaction vouchers. Each voucher covers one transaction fee. After using all vouchers, transactions cost only 0.01 USDST (~$0.01) each.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
What is bridging? - Move tokens from Ethereum mainnet to STRATO - Transfer tokens from STRATO back to Ethereum - Enables use of your existing assets on STRATO - Secure, decentralized cross-chain transfers

Why bridge to STRATO? - Lower fees: < $0.10 per transaction vs. $5-50+ on Ethereum - Faster: 1-2 second finality vs. 12+ seconds - DeFi access: Use assets for borrowing, swapping, liquidity - Earn rewards: Participate in STRATO DeFi ecosystem

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
Before bridging:

  • [ ] Web3 wallet installed (MetaMask recommended)
  • [ ] Assets on Ethereum mainnet (or destination chain)
  • [ ] ETH for Ethereum gas fees ($5-50 depending on congestion)
  • [ ] Small USDST for STRATO gas fees (if bridging back)
  • [ ] ~10-15 minutes for bridge completion

docs.title: From Ethereum to STRATO
docs.text:
Common bridgeable assets:

  • ETH → ETHST
  • USDC → USDST (on STRATO)
  • USDT → USDST (on STRATO)
  • WBTC → WBTCST
  • Other ERC20 tokens (check bridge interface for full list)

docs.title: Token Naming
docs.text:
• On Ethereum: Original name (ETH, USDC, WBTC)
  • On STRATO: May have ST suffix (ETHST, WBTCST) or same name
  • Same value: 1:1 peg (1 ETH on Ethereum = 1 ETHST on STRATO)

docs.title: Bridge Costs
docs.text: There are NO bridge fees. You only pay:

docs.title: Ethereum → STRATO
docs.text:
• Ethereum gas: $5-50+ (varies with network congestion)
  • STRATO transaction: < $0.10 in USDST (or free with vouchers)

Cost optimization:

  • Bridge during low gas hours (late night/weekends UTC)
  • Use gas trackers (etherscan.io/gastracker)
  • Bridge larger amounts to spread out Ethereum gas costs

docs.title: STRATO → Ethereum
docs.text:
• STRATO transaction: < $0.10 in USDST (or free with vouchers)
  • Ethereum gas: Paid when claiming on Ethereum

docs.title: Step 1: Prepare Your Wallet
docs.text:
1. Ensure MetaMask (or wallet) is installed and unlocked
  2. Connect to Ethereum Mainnet network

  3. Verify you have:

  4. Assets to bridge

  5. ETHST for gas fees (~$10-30 extra)

docs.title: Step 2: Go to Bridge
docs.text:
1. Navigate to Bridge section in STRATO app
  2. Or visit bridge interface directly
  3. Connect wallet (may need to switch to Ethereum network)

docs.title: Step 3: Select Bridge Direction
docs.text:
• From: Ethereum Mainnet
  • To: STRATO

docs.title: Step 4: Choose Asset and Amount
docs.text:
1. Select token to bridge from Ethereum (e.g., USDC)
  2. Enter amount to transfer

  3. Review:

  4. You send: Amount on Ethereum

  5. You receive: Amount on STRATO (minus fees)
  6. Estimated time: ~10-15 minutes
  7. Gas estimate: Current Ethereum gas cost

Minimum amounts: Check minimum bridge amount (typically ~$50-100)

Start Small

Bridge a small test amount first (~$100) to verify the process works before transferring larger amounts.

docs.title: Step 5: Initiate Bridge
docs.text:
1. Click Bridge or Transfer
  2. Review transaction details in wallet
  3. Check gas fee - if too high, wait for lower gas
  4. Confirm transaction
  5. Wait for Ethereum confirmation (12-60 seconds)

docs.title: Step 7: Wait for Bridge Completion
docs.text:
Timeline:

  • Ethereum confirmation: 12-60 seconds
  • Bridge processing: 5-10 minutes
  • STRATO arrival: Automatic

Track progress:

  • Bridge interface shows status
  • Check transaction hash on Etherscan
  • Monitor STRATO wallet balance

✅ Complete! Assets appear in STRATO wallet (15-20 minutes total).

docs.title: Step 1: Switch to STRATO Network
docs.text:
1. Open MetaMask
  2. Switch to STRATO network
  3. Verify you have assets to bridge + USDST for gas

docs.title: Step 2: Go to Bridge
docs.text:
1. Navigate to Bridge section
  2. Connect wallet (should be on STRATO)

docs.title: Step 3: Select Bridge Direction
docs.text:
• From: STRATO
  • To: Ethereum Mainnet

docs.title: Step 4: Choose Asset and Amount
docs.text:
1. Select token (e.g., ETHST → ETH)
  2. Enter amount
  3. Review details and fees

docs.title: Step 5: Initiate Bridge
docs.text:
1. Click Bridge
  2. Confirm on STRATO (< $0.10 gas in USDST)
  3. Wait for confirmation (1-2 seconds)

docs.title: Step 6: Claim on Ethereum
docs.text:
After STRATO transaction confirms:

  1. Wait for bridge processing (~10-15 minutes)
  2. Switch to Ethereum Mainnet in wallet
  3. Go to bridge interface → "Claim" section
  4. Click Claim [Token]
  5. Pay Ethereum gas fee to finalize
  6. Tokens arrive in Ethereum wallet

Two-Step Process

Bridging to Ethereum requires two transactions: one on STRATO (to initiate) and one on Ethereum (to claim). This is standard for cross-chain bridges.

docs.title: In-Progress Bridges
docs.text:
Check status:

  1. Go to Deposits (sidebar) → Bridge In tab → History OR go to Withdrawals (sidebar) → History
  2. View active transfers
  3. See estimated completion time
  4. Click transaction hash for blockchain explorer

Status indicators:

  • ⏳ Pending: Waiting for confirmations
  • 🔄 Processing: Bridge validators working
  • ✅ Complete: Assets transferred
  • ❌ Failed: Transaction reverted (rare)

docs.title: Bridge History
docs.text:
View all past bridges:

  • Date/time of transfer
  • Amount and token
  • Direction (Ethereum → STRATO or reverse)
  • Transaction hashes (both chains)
  • Status and completion

docs.title: "Bridge taking too long"
docs.text:
Normal duration: 10-20 minutes total

If delayed (> 30 minutes):

  • Verify Ethereum transaction confirmed (check Etherscan)
  • Ensure sufficient gas was paid
  • Check bridge status page for delays
  • Contact support with transaction hash

docs.title: "Insufficient ETHST for gas"
docs.text:
Cause: Not enough ETH to pay Ethereum gas fees

Fix:

  • Get more ETH to your Ethereum wallet
  • Wait for lower gas prices
  • Try again with sufficient ETHST balance

docs.title: "Transaction failed" / Reverted
docs.text:
Causes:

  • Gas limit too low
  • Token not supported
  • Amount below minimum
  • Smart contract error

Fix:

  • Check error message on Etherscan
  • Verify token is supported
  • Ensure amount > minimum
  • Try again or contact support

docs.title: "Cannot claim on Ethereum"
docs.text:
Cause: Bridge not fully processed yet

Fix:

  • Wait full 15-20 minutes from initiation
  • Refresh bridge page
  • Check if claim button appears
  • Verify you're on Ethereum network in wallet

docs.title: Before Bridging
docs.text:
• [ ] Check current Ethereum gas prices
  • [ ] Calculate total cost (gas fees only)
  • [ ] Verify sufficient balance (amount + gas)
  • [ ] Start with small test amount
  • [ ] Bookmark official bridge URL

docs.title: During Bridging
docs.text:
• [ ] Double-check token selection
  • [ ] Verify destination network
  • [ ] Review gas fee (cancel if too high)
  • [ ] Save transaction hashes
  • [ ] Don't close browser during process

docs.title: After Bridging
docs.text:
• [ ] Verify assets arrived
  • [ ] Check balance on destination chain
  • [ ] Save transaction records
  • [ ] Report any issues promptly

docs.title: Protect Yourself
docs.text:
• ✅ Verify URLs: Only use official STRATO bridge
  • ✅ Check token addresses: Ensure correct token contracts
  • ✅ Start small: Test with small amounts first
  • ✅ Save transaction hashes: For tracking and support

docs.title: Red Flags
docs.text:
• ❌ Unofficial bridge URLs
  • ❌ "Too good to be true" offers (airdrops, bonuses)
  • ❌ Requests for seed phrase (never share!)
  • ❌ Unverified token contracts

docs.title: Official Resources
docs.text:
• STRATO App: https://app.strato.nexus/
  • Support: support.blockapps.net
  • Telegram: t.me/strato_net

docs.title: When to Bridge
docs.text:
Best times for lower Ethereum gas:

  • Weekends (Saturday-Sunday)
  • Late night UTC (2-6 AM)
  • During low network activity

Check gas prices:

  • Etherscan Gas Tracker
  • Target: < 30 gwei for reasonable fees

docs.title: Batch Transfers
docs.text:
Instead of multiple small bridges:

  • Bridge larger amounts less frequently
  • Amortizes fixed costs across more value
  • Fewer approval transactions

Example:

  • ❌ Bridge $100 five times = 5× gas fees
  • ✅ Bridge $500 once = 1× gas fee

docs.title: Alternative Routes
docs.text:
For large amounts:

  • Consider centralized exchange (CEX) if available
  • Direct STRATO deposit from CEX
  • May save on Ethereum gas fees

docs.title: How It Works
docs.text:
1. Lock on Source: Tokens locked in source chain contract
  2. Validators Sign: Bridge validators confirm transaction
  3. Mint on Destination: Equivalent tokens minted on destination
  4. Unlock: Original tokens unlocked when bridging back

docs.title: Security Model
docs.text:
• Multi-sig validators: Multiple parties must approve
  • Time locks: Delays prevent instant theft
  • Audited contracts: Security-reviewed code
  • Decentralized: No single point of failure

docs.title: 1:1 Peg
docs.text:
• 1 ETH on Ethereum = 1 ETHST on STRATO
  • Value maintained across chains
  • Can always bridge back 1:1

docs.title: Start Using Your Assets
docs.text:
Now that assets are on STRATO:

  • Borrow USDST - Get liquidity against your bridged assets
  • Swap Tokens - Trade between different tokens
  • Provide Liquidity - Earn fees with your assets

docs.title: Explore More
docs.text:
• Quick Start Guide - Complete setup guide
  • Core Concepts - Understand DeFi fundamentals
  • Safety Guide - Security best practices

docs.title: Need Help?
docs.text:
• FAQ: Bridge Questions
  • Support: support.blockapps.net
  • API Reference: Interactive API (Swagger)
  • Telegram: t.me/strato_net

docs.title: Provide Liquidity
docs.text:
Earn fees by providing liquidity to STRATO's decentralized exchange pools.

Variable Returns & Risks

APR estimates, gas costs, and trading fees shown are based on historical data and typical conditions. Actual returns may vary significantly based on trading volume, pool size, and market conditions. You may also experience impermanent loss. Always research pool performance before providing liquidity.

docs.title: Complete Example: Provide $6,000 ETHST-USDST Liquidity
docs.text:
Your situation:

  • You have: 1 ETHST ($3,000) + 3,000 USDST
  • You want: Earn trading fees passively

What you'll do:

  1. Choose ETHST-USDST pool
  2. Add liquidity
  3. Earn fees automatically
  4. Remove liquidity (anytime)

Time needed: 5 minutes Potential earnings: ~8-15% APR (fees + Reward Points) Total gas cost: ~$0.30

docs.title: Quick Walkthrough
docs.text:
Step 1: Choose Pool - Go to Pools page - Select ETHST-USDST pool - Check: 24h volume $500k, APR 12%

Step 2: Enter Amounts - Enter: 1 ETHST - Auto-fills: 3,000 USDST (to match ratio) - Your share: 0.5% of pool

Step 3: Add Liquidity - Click "Add Liquidity" (~$0.10 gas) - Confirm in wallet - Approvals + add liquidity happen automatically in one transaction - Wait 1-2 seconds

Result: 

✅ Added: 1 ETHST + 3,000 USDST to pool
✅ Received: LP tokens (representing your 0.5% share)
✅ Earning: ~$2/day in fees (12% APR)
✅ Plus: Reward Points

Your position:

  • Pool share: 0.5%
  • Value: $6,000
  • Daily earnings: ~$2 in fees
  • Monthly: ~$60 (if volume stays constant)

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
What is liquidity providing? - Deposit token pairs into liquidity pools (e.g., ETHST + USDC) - Earn trading fees from every swap that uses your pool - Receive LP (Liquidity Provider) tokens representing your share - Plus earn Reward Points

Why provide liquidity? - Passive income: Earn from trading fees (typically 0.3% per trade) - Reward Points: Bonus rewards for liquidity providers - Support ecosystem: Enable trading for others - Capital efficiency: Put idle assets to work

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
Before providing liquidity:

  • [ ] STRATO account set up (Quick Start Guide)
  • [ ] Wallet connected to STRATO network
  • [ ] Both tokens of a pair in your wallet (e.g., ETHST + USDC)
  • [ ] Small amount of USDST for gas fees
  • [ ] Understanding of impermanent loss (Core Concepts)

docs.title: Automated Market Maker (AMM)
docs.text:
1. You deposit equal value of two tokens (e.g., $1,000 ETHST + $1,000 USDC)
  2. Traders swap between these tokens, paying fees
  3. You earn a share of fees proportional to your pool share
  4. You can withdraw anytime (subject to available liquidity)

docs.title: Pool Math
docs.text:
Pools maintain a constant product:

x × y = k (constant)

Where:
x = Token A reserves
y = Token B reserves  
k = constant product

This formula automatically sets prices based on supply and demand.

docs.title: Your Share
docs.text:
You receive LP tokens representing your ownership:

Your LP tokens / Total LP tokens = Your % of pool

Example: You own 1,000 LP tokens, total is 100,000 LP tokens - Your share: 1% - You earn: 1% of all trading fees

docs.title: Step 1: Choose a Pool
docs.text:
1. Navigate to Pools section in STRATO app
  2. Browse available pools or search for specific pair

  3. Check pool stats:

  4. TVL (Total Value Locked): Pool size

  5. Volume 24h: Trading activity
  6. APR: Estimated annual returns (fees + rewards)

Popular pools:

  • ETHST / USDST - High volume, stable
  • WBTC / ETHST - Dual crypto exposure
  • USDST / USDST - Minimal impermanent loss

docs.title: Step 2: Enter Amounts
docs.text:
1. Click Add Liquidity on chosen pool
  2. Enter amount for one token
  3. Second token amount auto-fills to maintain pool ratio

Example:

  • Pool is 50% ETHST, 50% USDST
  • You enter: 1 ETHST ($3,000)
  • Auto-fills: 3,000 USDST (to match value)

Start Small

Test with ~$100-500 first to understand the process before committing large amounts.

docs.title: Step 3: Review Details
docs.text:
Check:

  • Token amounts: Both tokens and their USD values
  • Pool share: Your % of total pool
  • Exchange rate: Current price in pool
  • Estimated APR: Expected annual returns
  • Fees: Gas cost (< $0.10 in USDST)

docs.title: Step 4: Add Liquidity
docs.text:
1. Click Add Liquidity
  2. Review transaction in wallet popup
  3. Confirm transaction
  4. Wait for confirmation (~1-2 seconds)

✅ Done! You now have LP tokens in your wallet.

docs.title: Trading Fees
docs.text:
How you earn:

  • Every swap pays 0.3% fee (typical)
  • Fees distributed proportionally to all LP holders
  • Automatically added to pool (compounds)

Example:

  • You own 1% of ETH-USDC pool
  • Pool does $1,000,000 daily volume
  • Daily fees: $1,000,000 × 0.3% = $3,000
  • Your share: $3,000 × 1% = $30/day

docs.title: Reward Points
docs.text:
Additional rewards in Reward Points:

  • Distributed to select pools
  • Varies by pool and season
  • Claim anytime in Rewards section

docs.title: Tracking Earnings
docs.text:
Monitor your position:

  1. Go to Advanced (in sidebar) → Swap Pools tab
  2. See your LP positions
  3. View accumulated fees
  4. Check current value vs. initial deposit

docs.title: Step 1: Go to Your Positions
docs.text:
1. Navigate to Portfolio → Liquidity
  2. Select pool to withdraw from

docs.title: Step 2: Choose Amount
docs.text:
• Remove All: Withdraw 100% of position
  • Partial: Enter % or amount to withdraw
  • Receive both tokens proportionally

docs.title: Step 3: Confirm Withdrawal
docs.text:
1. Click Remove Liquidity
  2. Review amounts you'll receive
  3. Confirm transaction in wallet
  4. Wait for confirmation

Tokens (including earned fees) return to your wallet.

docs.title: What Is It?
docs.text: Impermanent Loss (IL): Loss vs. simply holding the tokens when prices diverge.

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
Initial deposit:

  • 1 ETHST ($3,000) + 3,000 USDST = $6,000 total

ETH doubles to $6,000:

  • Pool rebalances: 0.707 ETHST + 4,242 USDST - Pool value: $8,485
  • If you just held: 1 ETHST ($6,000) + 3,000 USDST = $9,000
  • Impermanent loss: $515 (5.7%)

docs.title: When Is It a Problem?
docs.text:
• High IL: When token prices diverge significantly
  • Low IL: Stablecoin pairs (USDC-USDST) or correlated assets

docs.title: Mitigating IL
docs.text:
1. Choose stable pairs: USDC-USDST has minimal IL
  2. Earn fees to offset: High-volume pools compensate with fees
  3. Long-term provide: Price often reverts, making loss "impermanent"
  4. Reward Points: Additional rewards can exceed IL

IL is Impermanent

Loss only realizes if you withdraw. If prices revert, the "loss" disappears. Plus trading fees and rewards often exceed IL.

docs.title: Choosing Pools
docs.text:
Good pools have:

  • ✅ High trading volume (more fees earned)
  • ✅ Deep liquidity (lower IL from your deposits)
  • ✅ Verified tokens (avoid scams)
  • ✅ Reward Points incentives

Avoid pools with:

  • ❌ Unknown/unverified tokens
  • ❌ Extremely low liquidity
  • ❌ Suspicious APR (too good to be true)

docs.title: Managing IL Risk
docs.text:
Low IL strategies:

  • Stablecoin pairs (USDC-USDST, USDC-DAI)
  • Correlated assets (WBTC-ETH move together)
  • Short time horizons in stable markets

Higher IL but profitable:

  • High-volume pairs (fees offset IL)
  • Pools with Reward Points
  • Long-term positions (price reverts)

docs.title: Position Management
docs.text:
• [ ] Monitor positions weekly
  • [ ] Check if fees exceed IL
  • [ ] Rebalance between pools based on APR
  • [ ] Claim Reward Points regularly
  • [ ] Withdraw if IL becomes too large

docs.title: TVL (Total Value Locked)
docs.text: Total $ value in the pool. Higher = more stable prices, lower price impact.

docs.title: Volume 24h
docs.text: Total trading volume in last 24 hours. Higher = more fees earned.

docs.title: APR (Annual Percentage Rate)
docs.text:
Estimated yearly returns from:

  • Trading fees
  • Reward Points

Example: 25% APR on $10,000 = ~$2,500/year

Warning

APR is estimated based on recent activity. Actual returns vary with volume and prices.

docs.title: Your Pool Share
docs.text:
Your % of total pool. Determines your fee earnings:

Your fees = Total pool fees × Your share %

docs.title: Liquidity Farming
docs.text:
Maximize returns by:

  1. Choose highest APR pools
  2. Reinvest earned fees (compound)
  3. Claim and reinvest Reward Points
  4. Rotate to best-performing pools

docs.title: Range Orders (Concentrated Liquidity)
docs.text:
Some pools support concentrated liquidity:

  • Provide liquidity in specific price ranges
  • Earn more fees per capital
  • Higher risk if price exits range

Not yet available on all STRATO pools - check pool type.

docs.title: Arbitrage Protection
docs.text:
Be aware of:

  • Price discrepancies between exchanges
  • Arbitrageurs quickly correct, earning fees from your pool
  • You still earn fees, but IL can occur rapidly

docs.title: "Insufficient Balance"
docs.text:
Cause: Not enough of one or both tokens

Fix:

  • Check you have both tokens
  • Verify balances cover amounts + gas
  • Swap to get missing token

docs.title: "Price Updated" Warning
docs.text:
Cause: Pool ratio changed since you started

Fix:

  • Accept new price
  • Or cancel and try again with updated amounts

docs.title: "Slippage Tolerance Exceeded"
docs.text:
Cause: Pool price moved beyond tolerance

Fix:

  • Increase slippage in settings
  • Try again with updated amounts

docs.title: Diversification
docs.text:
• Don't put all capital in one pool
  • Split across 3-5 pools
  • Mix stable and volatile pairs

docs.title: Start Small
docs.text:
• Test with $100-500 first
  • Learn the mechanics
  • Scale up gradually

docs.title: Monitor Regularly
docs.text:
• Check positions weekly
  • Compare returns vs. holding
  • Exit if IL exceeds comfort level

docs.title: Emergency Exit
docs.text:
If you need to exit quickly:

  • Remove liquidity anytime
  • Receive both tokens back
  • May realize impermanent loss
  • But you keep all earned fees

docs.title: Claim Your Rewards
docs.text:
Maximize earnings:

→ Rewards Guide - Learn to claim Reward Points

docs.title: Optimize Your Portfolio
docs.text:
• Swap Guide - Rebalance between pools
  • Borrow USDST - Leverage your LP positions
  • Core Concepts - Deepen your understanding

docs.title: Need Help?
docs.text:
• FAQ: Common Questions
  • Support: support.blockapps.net
  • API Reference: Interactive API (Swagger)
  • Telegram: t.me/strato_net

docs.title: Mint USDST via CDP
docs.text:
Create USDST stablecoin by depositing collateral into a CDP (Collateralized Debt Position).

Alternative: Borrow from Lending Pool

You can also borrow USDST from the lending pool which offers more flexibility but typically has higher interest rates.

Variable Parameters

Stability fees, gas costs, and collateralization requirements shown are typical examples. Actual values may vary based on network conditions, asset type, and governance settings. Always check current parameters in the app before transacting.

docs.title: Complete Example: Mint $1,000 USDST
docs.text:
Your situation:

  • You have: 1 ETHST in your wallet
  • ETHST price: $3,000
  • You need: $1,000 USDST long-term

What you'll do:

  1. Deposit 1 ETHST into CDP vault
  2. Mint 1,000 USDST
  3. Use your USDST
  4. Burn USDST to repay (anytime)
  5. Withdraw your collateral

Time needed: 5 minutes Total gas cost: ~$0.30 (3 transactions) Stability fee: ~2-3% annually (lower than lending)

docs.title: CDP vs Lending: Which to Choose?
docs.text:
Feature CDP (Minting) Lending Pool (Borrowing) Action Mint new USDST Borrow from pool Fees Stability fee (~2-3% annually) Interest (~5% annually) Best for Long-term positions (months) Short-term needs (days/weeks) Metric Collateralization Ratio (CR) Health Factor Min Ratio 150% (varies by asset) Depends on liquidation threshold Flexibility More capital efficient Easier to manage

Choose CDP if:

  • ✅ You want lower fees
  • ✅ Long-term position (months+)
  • ✅ Maximizing capital efficiency
  • ✅ Comfortable managing vaults

Choose Lending if:

  • ✅ Short-term liquidity need
  • ✅ Want simpler management
  • ✅ Frequent changes to position
  • ✅ See Borrow Guide

docs.title: Step 1: Deposit Collateral
docs.text:
What you have:

  • 1 ETHST worth $3,000

In the app:

  1. Go to Advanced (in sidebar) → "Mint" tab (Vaults sub-tab is default)
  2. In the Mint Widget:
  3. Select collateral asset: Choose ETH
  4. Enter deposit amount: Type 1.0 (or click "Max")
  5. Leave borrow amount empty for now
  6. Click the action button (will show "Deposit")
  7. Confirm in wallet (~$0.10 gas)
  8. Approval + deposit happen automatically in one transaction
  9. Wait 1-2 seconds

Result: 

✅ Deposited: 1 ETHST ($3,000) into CDP vault
✅ Can mint up to: $2,000 USDST (150% min CR)
✅ Collateralization Ratio: N/A (no debt yet)

Your wallet:

  • Before: 1 ETHST
  • After: 0 ETHST (moved to CDP vault)

docs.title: Step 2: Mint USDST
docs.text:
What you want: Mint $1,000 USDST

In the app:

  1. Go to Advanced (in sidebar) → "Mint" tab → Vaults sub-tab
  2. In the Mint Widget:
  3. Your existing collateral shows automatically
  4. Enter borrow amount: Type 1000 USDST

  5. Review the preview:

  6. Minting: 1,000 USDST

  7. New Collateralization Ratio: 300% (Very safe ✓)
  8. Stability Fee: ~2-3% annually
  9. Min required CR: 150%
  10. Click "Mint USDST"
  11. Confirm in wallet (~$0.10 gas)
  12. Wait 1-2 seconds

Result: 

✅ Minted: 1,000 USDST (created new tokens)
✅ Collateralization Ratio: 300% (Very safe)
✅ Your wallet: +1,000 USDST

Your position now:

  • Collateral: 1 ETHST ($3,000)
  • Debt: 1,000 USDST
  • Collateralization Ratio (CR): 300%
  • Min required CR: 150%
  • Still can mint: ~$1,000 more (but don't!)

What is Collateralization Ratio? 

CR = (Collateral Value / Debt) × 100%
   = ($3,000 / $1,000) × 100% = 300%

  • Above 200%: Very safe ✅
  • 150-200%: Moderate risk ⚠️
  • Below 150%: Liquidation danger ❌

Your 300% CR means you have a huge safety buffer.

docs.title: Step 3: Use Your USDST
docs.text:
You now have 1,000 USDST to use for:

  • ✅ Transaction fees on STRATO
  • ✅ Swap for other tokens
  • ✅ Provide liquidity
  • ✅ Bridge to other chains
  • ✅ Any other purpose

Your debt grows slowly:

  • Stability fee: ~2-3% per year
  • After 30 days: Owe ~$1,002
  • After 1 year: Owe ~$1,025

Lower fees than lending:

  • CDP: ~$2-3 per month on $1,000
  • Lending: ~$4-5 per month on $1,000
  • Savings: ~40% lower fees

docs.title: Step 4: Burn USDST (Repay Anytime)
docs.text:
When you're ready to close or reduce your position:

  1. Get USDST to burn:

  2. You might still have what you minted

  3. Or swap other tokens for USDST

  4. Or borrow from lending pool

  5. In the Mint Widget:

  6. Enter repay amount: How much USDST to burn

  7. Enter amount:

  8. Type specific amount (e.g., 1002 to close completely)

  9. Or click "Burn Max" to burn all debt
  10. Click "Burn USDST"
  11. Confirm in wallet (~$0.10 gas)
  12. Wait 1-2 seconds

Result after full burn: 

✅ Burned: 1,002 USDST (destroyed tokens)
✅ Debt repaid: 1,000 principal + 2 stability fee
✅ CR: Infinite (no debt)
✅ Gas cost: ~$0.10

Your position:

  • Collateral: 1 ETHST (still in vault)
  • Debt: 0 USDST
  • CR: No debt
  • You can now withdraw collateral

docs.title: Step 5: Withdraw Collateral
docs.text:
After burning all debt:

  1. In your vault (shown below the Mint Widget)
  2. Enter amount: Type 1.0 (or click "Withdraw Max")
  3. Click "Withdraw"
  4. Confirm in wallet (~$0.10 gas)
  5. Wait 1-2 seconds

Result: 

✅ Withdrawn: 1 ETHST to your wallet
✅ Vault closed

Final accounting:

  • You minted: 1,000 USDST
  • You burned: 1,002 USDST
  • Total cost: $2 stability fee + $0.30 gas = $2.30 total
  • You still have: 1 ETHST (same as you started)

docs.title: Scenario: ETHST Drops to $2,200
docs.text:
What happens:

  • Your collateral value: Now $2,200 (was $3,000)
  • Your debt: Still 1,000 USDST (unchanged)
  • Your CR: Drops to 220% (still safe ✓)

What to do:

  • Option 1: Monitor - Still above 150% minimum
  • Option 2: Add collateral - Deposit more ETHST to increase CR
  • Option 3: Burn some debt - Burn 200 USDST to boost CR

docs.title: Scenario: ETHST Drops to $1,600 (Danger!)
docs.text:
What happens:

  • Your collateral value: Now $1,600
  • Your debt: Still 1,000 USDST
  • Your CR: 160% (approaching minimum ⚠️)

Danger zone:

  • CR below 150% = you can be liquidated
  • Liquidators can take your collateral + 5-10% penalty
  • You lose ETHST value beyond your debt

What to do immediately:

  1. Add more collateral (safer), OR
  2. Burn some debt (faster)
  3. Keep CR above 200% for safety

Best practice: Keep CR above 200% for peace of mind.

docs.title: Check Your Vault
docs.text:
In the app:

  • Go to Advanced (in sidebar) → Mint tab → My Vaults

  • You'll see:

  • Collateral amount and value

  • Debt amount (with accrued fees)
  • Collateralization Ratio with indicator
  • Available to mint or withdraw

CR indicators:

  • 🟢 Green (> 200%): Safe
  • 🟡 Yellow (150-200%): Caution
  • 🔴 Red (< 150%): Danger - liquidation risk

docs.title: Adding More Collateral
docs.text:
If CR drops:

  1. Go to Deposit
  2. Add more collateral
  3. CR improves immediately

docs.title: Partial Burn
docs.text:
Don't need to burn all at once:

  1. Go to Burn
  2. Enter any amount
  3. Reduces debt and improves CR

docs.title: Mint More USDST
docs.text:
If you need more USDST and have room:

  1. Check current CR
  2. If above 200%, can safely mint more
  3. Go to Mint → Enter amount
  4. CR will decrease

docs.title: Multiple Vaults
docs.text:
You can have one vault per collateral type:

Example:

  • ETHST Vault: 2 ETHST deposited, 2,000 USDST minted
  • WBTC Vault: 0.1 WBTC deposited, 3,000 USDST minted

Each vault:

  • Has its own CR
  • Is managed separately
  • Can be liquidated independently

Combined view:

  • Total collateral value: $10,000
  • Total debt: 5,000 USDST
  • Overall CR: 200%

docs.title: DO ✅
docs.text:
• Over-collateralize: Keep CR above 200%
  • Monitor daily: Check CR when prices move
  • Set alerts: Use price alerts for your collateral
  • Long-term use: CDP works best for months+ positions
  • Diversify vaults: Spread across different collateral
  • Track fees: Stability fees are lower but still accrue

docs.title: DON'T ❌
docs.text:
• Min CR trap: Don't mint at exactly 150% CR
  • Ignore warnings: Yellow/red CR = take action
  • Forget fees: Even 2-3% adds up over time
  • Mix up debt: Track which vault has which debt
  • Panic close: Can add collateral instead
  • Forget gas: Keep USDST for fees

docs.title: "Insufficient collateral"
docs.text:
Problem: Trying to mint more than your CR allows

Solution:

  1. Deposit more collateral first, OR
  2. Reduce the mint amount

docs.title: "Below debt floor"
docs.text:
Problem: Trying to mint less than minimum required debt

Solution:

  • Each asset has minimum debt (e.g., 100 USDST)
  • Mint at least that amount
  • Or close vault completely by burning all

docs.title: "Would violate minimum CR"
docs.text:
Problem: This action would make your CR too low

Solution:

  • If minting: Reduce amount or add collateral
  • If withdrawing: Reduce amount or burn some debt
  • Keep buffer above 150% minimum

docs.title: "Insufficient USDST balance"
docs.text:
Problem: Don't have enough USDST to burn

Solution:

  1. Swap other tokens for USDST, OR
  2. Borrow USDST from lending pool temporarily, OR
  3. Burn a smaller amount now, rest later

docs.title: CR dropping
docs.text:
Problem: Your collateral value is decreasing

Solution (act quickly):

  1. Deposit more collateral (safest)
  2. Burn some debt (also good)
  3. Monitor closely if still above 200%
  4. Don't wait until you're near 150%

docs.title: Stability Fees
docs.text:
How fees work:

  • Fees accrue every second
  • Typical rate: ~2-3% annually
  • Lower than lending pool rates
  • Compounds continuously

Example costs: | Minted | Time | Fee Owed | |--------|------|----------| | $1,000 | 1 day | $0.08 | | $1,000 | 1 week | $0.50 | | $1,000 | 30 days | $2 | | $1,000 | 1 year | $25 | | $10,000 | 30 days | $20 |

Compare to Lending: | Amount | Time | CDP Fee | Lending Interest | Savings | |--------|------|---------|------------------|---------| | $1,000 | 30 days | $2 | $4 | $2 (50%) | | $1,000 | 1 year | $25 | $50 | $25 (50%) | | $10,000 | 1 year | $250 | $500 | $250 (50%) |

docs.title: Gas Fees
docs.text:
Action Gas Cost Deposit collateral ~$0.10 Mint USDST ~$0.10 Burn USDST ~$0.10 Withdraw collateral ~$0.10

Total for complete cycle: ~$0.30-$0.40

docs.title: Example: $10,000 Position for 6 Months
docs.text:
CDP:

  • Collateral: 5 ETHST ($10,000)
  • Minted: 5,000 USDST
  • CR: 200%
  • Stability fee: 2.5% annually
  • Cost: $62.50 for 6 months

Lending:

  • Collateral: 5 ETHST ($10,000)
  • Borrowed: 5,000 USDST
  • Health Factor: 1.2
  • Interest: 5% annually
  • Cost: $125 for 6 months

Savings with CDP: $62.50 (50% less)

docs.title: Use CDP (Minting) When:
docs.text:
✅ Long-term position (6+ months) ✅ Want lowest fees ✅ Maximizing capital efficiency ✅ Comfortable managing CR ✅ Planning to hold position

Example: You want to mint USDST to provide liquidity for a year

docs.title: Use Lending (Borrowing) When:
docs.text:
✅ Short-term need (days/weeks) ✅ Want simple management ✅ Frequent position changes ✅ Testing strategies ✅ Need flexibility

Example: You need USDST for a quick trade this week

docs.title: Maximum Leverage Example
docs.text:
Conservative (Recommended):

  • Deposit: 2 ETHST ($6,000)
  • Mint: 2,000 USDST
  • CR: 300%
  • Very safe ✓

Moderate:

  • Deposit: 1.5 ETHST ($4,500)
  • Mint: 2,000 USDST
  • CR: 225%
  • Safe with buffer

Aggressive (Not Recommended):

  • Deposit: 1.2 ETHST ($3,600)
  • Mint: 2,000 USDST
  • CR: 180%
  • Risky - small price drop = danger

Never go below 200% CR in volatile markets!
