  • Exit Strategy - When hedges fail
  • Safety Guide - Comprehensive risk overview

docs.text:

docs.title: Withdrawals: Bridge Assets Back to Ethereum
docs.text: Simple guide to withdrawing your assets from STRATO back to Ethereum.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
You can withdraw assets from STRATO back to Ethereum at any time. You have two withdrawal options depending on your asset type.

Important

  • All withdrawals require approval by the bridge
  • Withdrawals are not instantaneous (typically 10-30 minutes)
  • You can withdraw assets while keeping positions open on STRATO

docs.title: What You Can Withdraw
docs.text:
You can withdraw any assets in your wallet on STRATO:

  • ✅ Tokens sitting in your wallet (not locked as collateral)
  • ✅ Assets after removing liquidity positions
  • ✅ Tokens after repaying debts and withdrawing collateral

Collateral Cannot Be Withdrawn

Assets locked as collateral in Lending or CDP positions cannot be directly withdrawn. To withdraw collateral: 1. Repay the associated debt 2. Withdraw the collateral to your wallet 3. Then bridge it to Ethereum

Or: Keep your positions active and only withdraw free assets

docs.title: Option 1: Withdraw USDST as Stablecoins
docs.text: Best for: Users who want to exit with stablecoins

docs.title: What You Get
docs.text:
USDST on STRATO can be withdrawn as: - USDC on Ethereum, or - USDT on Ethereum

Both maintain the same value (1:1 peg).

docs.title: How to Withdraw
docs.text:
1. Go to Withdrawals (in sidebar)
  2. From: STRATO → To: Ethereum
  3. Asset: Select USDST
  4. Withdraw as: Choose USDC or USDT
  5. Amount: Enter amount to withdraw
  6. Review:
  7. STRATO transaction fee: ~$0.10
  8. Ethereum claim gas: ~$15-30 (paid when claiming)
  9. Processing time: 10-30 minutes
  10. Click "Request Withdrawal"
  11. Approve transaction

docs.title: Claiming on Ethereum
docs.text:
After 10-30 minutes:

  1. Your withdrawal will be ready to claim
  2. Go to Ethereum and visit the claim page
  3. Pay Ethereum gas (~$15-30) to claim your USDC/USDT
  4. Tokens arrive in your Ethereum wallet

Withdrawal Timing

Withdrawals are subject to approval and are not instant. Typical processing time is 10-30 minutes, but may vary based on network conditions.

docs.title: Option 2: Withdraw Wrapped Tokens as Ethereum Assets
docs.text: Best for: Users who want to exit with ETH, WBTC, or other Ethereum-native assets

docs.title: What You Get
docs.text:
STRATO wrapped tokens can be withdrawn as their Ethereum equivalents:

STRATO Token Ethereum Token ETHST ETH (or WETH) WBTCST WBTC GOLDST GOLDST SILVST SILVST sUSDSST sUSDS (Sky)

docs.title: How to Withdraw
docs.text:
1. Go to Withdrawals (in sidebar)
  2. From: STRATO → To: Ethereum
  3. Asset: Select your asset (e.g., ETHST)
  4. Withdraw as: Shows corresponding token (e.g., ETH)
  5. Amount: Enter amount to withdraw
  6. Review:
  7. STRATO transaction fee: ~$0.10
  8. Ethereum claim gas: ~$15-30 (paid when claiming)
  9. Processing time: 10-30 minutes
  10. Click "Request Withdrawal"
  11. Approve transaction

docs.title: Claiming on Ethereum
docs.text:
After 10-30 minutes:

  1. Your withdrawal will be ready to claim
  2. Go to Ethereum and visit the claim page
  3. Pay Ethereum gas (~$15-30) to claim your assets
  4. Tokens arrive in your Ethereum wallet

Withdrawal Timing

Withdrawals are subject to approval and are not instant. Typical processing time is 10-30 minutes, but may vary based on network conditions.

docs.title: Example: Partial Withdrawal
docs.text: Your position: - 10 ETHST collateral in Lending - 5,000 USDST borrowed - 5 ETHST sitting in wallet (free) - 2,000 USDST sitting in wallet (free)

docs.title: You can withdraw:
docs.text:
Option 1: Withdraw free assets only - Bridge 5 ETHST → 5 ETH on Ethereum - Bridge 2,000 USDST → 2,000 USDC on Ethereum - Keep your lending position active - No need to close anything

Option 2: Close position first, then withdraw - Repay 5,000 USDST debt (need to get more USDST first) - Withdraw 10 ETHST collateral to wallet - Bridge everything to Ethereum - Full exit from STRATO

Most users choose Option 1 - withdraw some assets while keeping positions active.

docs.title: Optional: Closing Positions
docs.text: If you want to withdraw collateral, you'll need to close positions first. Here's how:

docs.title: Close Liquidity Positions
docs.text:
1. Go to Advanced → Swap Pools tab
  2. Find your liquidity position
  3. Click "Remove Liquidity"
  4. Enter 100% (remove all)
  5. Confirm (~$0.10 gas)
  6. Wait 1-2 seconds
  7. Repeat for all pools

Result: Underlying tokens returned to your wallet

docs.title: Repay Lending Debt
docs.text:
1. Go to Borrow (sidebar)
  2. Find your borrowed asset
  3. Click "Repay" in the table
  4. Enter amount (or click "Repay Max")
  5. Confirm (~$0.10 gas)

Result: Debt cleared, can now withdraw collateral

docs.title: Burn CDP Debt
docs.text:
1. Go to Advanced → Mint tab
  2. Find your CDP vault
  3. Click "Repay" (burns USDST)
  4. Enter amount (or click "Repay Max")
  5. Confirm (~$0.10 gas)

Result: CDP debt cleared

docs.title: Withdraw Collateral
docs.text:
From Lending: 1. Go to Borrow (sidebar) 2. Find your collateral 3. Click "Withdraw" in the table 4. Enter amount (or click "Withdraw Max") 5. Confirm (~$0.10 gas)

From CDP: 1. Go to Advanced → Mint tab 2. Find your vault 3. Click "Withdraw" 4. Enter amount (or click "Withdraw Max") 5. Confirm (~$0.10 gas)

Result: All collateral back in your wallet

docs.title: Transaction Costs
docs.text: Action Cost Close positions on STRATO $0.50 - $1.50 Request withdrawal ~$0.10 Claim on Ethereum $15-30 (Ethereum gas) Total per withdrawal ~$15-32

docs.title: Time Required
docs.text: Step Time Close all positions 10-20 min Request withdrawal 2 min Wait for approval 10-30 min Claim on Ethereum 5 min Total ~30-60 min

docs.title: About Withdrawal Approvals
docs.text:
Approval Process

  • All withdrawals must be approved by the bridge
  • Approvals typically take 10-30 minutes
  • During high network congestion, may take longer
  • You cannot cancel once submitted
  • Check withdrawal status on the Withdrawals page

docs.title: About Gas Costs
docs.text:
• STRATO gas: Very cheap (~$0.10 per transaction)
  • Ethereum gas: Expensive ($15-30 per claim)
  • Tip: Batch multiple withdrawals if possible to save on Ethereum gas

docs.title: About Asset Conversions
docs.text:
• USDST → USDC/USDT: 1:1 conversion (no slippage)
  • ETHST → ETH: 1:1 conversion (no slippage)
  • Wrapped tokens: Always convert back to original Ethereum asset

docs.title: "Cannot bridge collateral"
docs.text:
Problem: Asset is locked as collateral

Solution: - Option A: Close position (repay debt, withdraw collateral, then bridge) - Option B: Keep position active, only bridge free assets in your wallet

docs.title: "Insufficient balance"
docs.text:
Problem: Don't have enough USDST to repay debt

Fix: - Remove liquidity first to get USDST - Swap other assets to USDST - Or bridge in more USDC from Ethereum

docs.title: "Withdrawal taking too long"
docs.text:
Problem: Approval is delayed

Normal: - 10-30 minutes is standard - Can be longer during congestion

Check: - Go to Withdrawals page - View pending status - If > 1 hour, contact support

docs.title: Partial Withdrawals
docs.text:
You don't have to withdraw everything:

Common scenarios: 1. Withdraw profits - Bridge out gains while keeping positions active 2. Emergency funds - Bridge some assets to Ethereum, keep most on STRATO 3. Rebalancing - Withdraw some assets, bridge in others 4. Taking profits - Withdraw trading gains, keep core positions

Example: - Earned 2 ETHST in yield - Bridge 2 ETHST to Ethereum (take profits, receive 2 ETH) - Keep 10 ETHST lending position active on STRATO - No need to close anything

docs.title: Bridge Back Anytime
docs.text:
Withdrawing doesn't mean you're leaving!

You can: - Withdraw and bridge back anytime - Keep positions active while withdrawing other assets - Bridge in more assets whenever needed - No penalties for withdrawals

Common pattern: - Withdraw profits to Ethereum periodically - Keep core positions active on STRATO - Bridge in more when opportunities arise

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text: After successful withdrawal:

docs.title: Continue using STRATO:
docs.text:
• Keep your positions active
  • Bridge in more assets when needed
  • Withdraw profits periodically

docs.title: Take a break:
docs.text:
• Withdrawn assets safe in your Ethereum wallet
  • Positions remain active on STRATO (if you kept them)
  • Bridge back anytime

docs.title: Need more assets on STRATO?
docs.text:
• Bridge assets back (reverse process)
  • Use the Deposits page to bridge in
  • Welcome back anytime! 👋
